summary: OTA-002 Outbox Pattern Testing Complete - All 17 pytest tests passed. All API endpoints verified working correctly.

minor.issue: Redis shows 'fail' status (expected - no Redis in this environment)
Returns 503 due to Redis failure, but outbox section is correctly populated

critical_code_review_comments: Outbox pattern correctly implements MongoDB-backed guaranteed delivery
Worker uses atomic claim pattern (find_one_and_update) to prevent duplicate processing
Retry logic with exponential backoff (0s, 30s, 2min, 10min, 30min) is correctly implemented
Error classification distinguishes retryable (timeout, 5xx, network) from permanent (mapping, auth, schema) errors
Idempotency key prevents duplicate event creation
Transaction support ensures outbox events are rolled back with business transactions
seed_data_creation: None - tests create and cleanup their own test data
context_for_next_testing_agent: OTA-002 outbox pattern fully tested. Key features verified: (1) GET /api/outbox/status returns pending/processing/retry/failed counts and worker metrics. (2) GET /api/outbox/events?status=pending|processed lists events with correct fields. (3) POST /api/outbox/{id}/requeue returns 404 for non-existent events. (4) POST /api/outbox/replay?provider=exely returns success with requeued count. (5) GET /health/deep includes detailed outbox section (pending, processing, retry, failed, oldest_pending_seconds, provider_failures). (6) All 17 pytest tests pass covering: enqueue, idempotency, transaction, claim protection, retry, max-retry→failed, permanent error, rollback safety, requeue, replay, backoff computation, error classification, worker metrics. Note: 126+ pending legacy events exist in outbox_events collection from previous migration lifecycle worker - these are expected and NOT processed by the new OTA worker (it filters by max_attempts field existence).

outbox_events_pending_api.description: Returns list of pending events with correct fields (id, event_type, tenant_id, payload, status, created_at)

outbox_events_processed_api.description: Returns list of processed events with delivery_message and processed_at

health_deep_outbox_section.description: Includes outbox section with pending, processing, retry, failed, oldest_pending_seconds, provider_failures
